message:
fix(cli): fix set -e bugs in support-bundle redaction + add tests (bd-31ps.2.2)

Fix three bash set -e safety bugs in redact_file/redact_bundle:
- ((counter++)) returns 1 when counter=0, aborting under set -e
- grep -qP '\x00' silently skips binary data; need -a flag
- [[ ]] && as last function statement returns 1 when false

Add comprehensive test suite (29 tests) covering API keys, GitHub
tokens, service tokens, Bearer/JWT, generic secrets, false positive
prevention, binary file skipping, bundle redaction, and --no-redact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-31sj","title":"Deep exploration: CASS (Coding Agent Session Search)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of CASS (Coding Agent Session Search) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify CASS installation
[[ -d /dp/coding_agent_session_search ]] && echo "PASS: cass repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: cass repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v cass &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: cass command available" || { echo "FAIL: cass not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Verify session data exists
[[ -d ~/.claude/projects ]] && echo "PASS: Claude projects directory exists" || echo "WARN: No session data yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/cass-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/coding_agent_session_search/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for search syntax docs, session format specs

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Session discovery across ~/.claude/projects/
  - Index building (SQLite FTS5) and search mechanisms
  - Output formats (--robot mode JSON)
  - Session metadata extraction (timestamps, tools used, context)
  - How it handles large session histories

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
cass --help 2>&1 | head -20
cass search --help 2>&1 | head -20
cass index --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
cass search "test" --limit 1 --robot 2>&1 | head -5
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'cass OR session search OR coding agent'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'cass session mining' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions (meta\!)

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/coding_agent_session_search/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/coding_agent_session_search && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Session discovery mechanism (glob patterns, directory traversal)
- [ ] Index structure and storage (SQLite FTS5 schema)
- [ ] Search query syntax (FTS5 operators, phrase matching)
- [ ] Robot mode output schema (JSON fields)
- [ ] Performance on large histories (indexing time, query speed)

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] cm (CASS Memory) - imports cass for session mining
- [ ] xf - similar search patterns
- [ ] mail - message search comparison
- [ ] ntm - session management

### 2.3 Claim Verification
```bash
# Verify session discovery claims
find ~/.claude/projects -name "*.jsonl" 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "Total session files found"

# Verify FTS5 capability
sqlite3 ~/.local/share/cass/sessions.db ".schema" 2>/dev/null | grep -i fts
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update cass entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Accurate one-liner from README
- `description`: Session discovery, indexing, search
- `deepDescription`: How it traverses ~/.claude/projects/
- `features`: Verified search capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test cass entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const cass = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'cass');
console.log('Testing cass entry...');
console.assert(cass, 'cass entry exists');
console.assert(cass.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(cass.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.assert(cass.connectsTo?.length > 0, 'has connections');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Full Search Pipeline
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/cass-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== CASS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Index build/refresh
echo "Test 1: Index operation..." | tee -a $LOG
cass index --force 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Basic search
echo "Test 2: Basic search..." | tee -a $LOG
cass search "function" --limit 5 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 3: Robot mode output
echo "Test 3: Robot mode JSON..." | tee -a $LOG
cass search "test" --robot --limit 3 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool | tee -a $LOG

# Test 4: Phrase search
echo "Test 4: Phrase search..." | tee -a $LOG
cass search '"error handling"' --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-31sj --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Content matches verified tool capabilities
- [ ] Synergies are reciprocal (cass->cm and cm->cass)
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:00:50.325460661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T21:31:11.109814287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-331g","title":"Dark mode toggle in web wizard","description":"## Overview
Add dark mode toggle to the web wizard with system preference detection and localStorage persistence.

## Current Problem
- Web wizard is light-mode only
- Users coding at night prefer dark mode
- No respect for system preference
- Common feature expectation in 2026

## Proposed Behavior
1. Default: Follow system preference (prefers-color-scheme)
2. Toggle: Manual override in header
3. Persist: Save preference to localStorage
4. Instant: No flash of wrong theme on load

## Implementation Details
1. Use CSS custom properties for colors
2. Add `dark` class to `<html>` element
3. Toggle button in header (sun/moon icon)
4. Script in `<head>` to prevent flash

## Color Tokens
```css
:root {
  --bg-primary: #ffffff;
  --text-primary: #1a1a1a;
  --accent: #3b82f6;
}

:root.dark {
  --bg-primary: #0a0a0a;
  --text-primary: #fafafa;
  --accent: #60a5fa;
}
```

## Flash Prevention
```html
<script>
  // In <head>, before CSS loads
  const theme = localStorage.getItem('theme') ||
    (matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches ? 'dark' : 'light');
  document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark', theme === 'dark');
</script>
```

## Toggle Component
```tsx
function ThemeToggle() {
  const [theme, setTheme] = useTheme();
  return (
    <button onClick={() => setTheme(theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark')}>
      {theme === 'dark' ? <SunIcon /> : <MoonIcon />}
    </button>
  );
}
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test system preference detection
- [ ] Test toggle persists across refreshes
- [ ] Test no flash on page load
- [ ] Test all components render correctly in both modes
- [ ] Visual regression tests

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/app/layout.tsx (theme script)
- apps/web/app/globals.css (color tokens)
- apps/web/components/ui/theme-toggle.tsx (new)
- All components using hardcoded colors","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:33.391611994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:10:13.490113278Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-331g","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:43.692504127Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-33vh","title":"EPIC: Complete Removal of Legacy git_safety_guard System","description":"## Background

The old Python-based `git_safety_guard` system has been replaced by DCG (Destructive Command Guard), a high-performance Rust-based solution. However, remnants of the old system still exist in the codebase and cause confusion for users.

## Historical Context

- **December 17, 2025**: An AI agent ran `git checkout --` on files containing hours of uncommitted work. This incident made clear that AGENTS.md instructions don't prevent execution—mechanical enforcement is needed.
- **December 21, 2025**: git_safety_guard.py introduced (commit e47496c)
- **December 29, 2025**: Enhanced to 508 lines (commit 92f362c)
- **January 11, 2026**: Completely removed in favor of DCG (commit f1fd501)

## Problem Statement

Users running `acfs doctor` on older installations see:
```
⚠ WARN Git safety guard
      Fix: mkdir -p ~/.claude/hooks && cp ~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py ~/.claude/hooks/ && chmod +x ~/.claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py
```

This fix command references files that NO LONGER EXIST. The check suggests copying from `~/.acfs/claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.py` but that path was removed from the repo.

## Root Cause Analysis

1. **install.sh lines 3937-3940**: Still has log messages saying 'Claude Git Safety Guard' even though it now calls configure_dcg()
2. **docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md**: References old system at lines 223 and 245
3. **User's deployed ~/.acfs/ directory**: Old doctor.sh on existing VPS installations still checks for git_safety_guard.py
4. **No migration path**: Users who installed before Jan 11, 2026 have stale checks

## Why DCG Replaced git_safety_guard

| Aspect | git_safety_guard (old) | DCG (new) |
|--------|------------------------|-----------|
| Language | Python | Rust |
| Performance | ~100ms startup | Sub-microsecond |
| Dependencies | Python runtime | Single binary |
| Features | Basic regex | SIMD-accelerated, modular packs |
| Maintenance | Part of ACFS | Dedicated project |
| Extensibility | Hardcoded | database, kubernetes, cloud packs |

## Scope

- Remove ALL git_safety_guard references from source code
- Update documentation to only reference DCG
- Ensure clean migration for existing users
- Update install.sh messaging

## Success Criteria

1. `grep -ri 'git_safety_guard' .` returns only historical .beads/ entries
2. `grep -ri 'Git Safety Guard' .` returns nothing
3. acfs doctor only checks for DCG
4. Documentation accurately reflects DCG as the sole safety mechanism
5. Existing users can upgrade cleanly without stale warnings","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:36:42.833192147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:36:42.833192147Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","dcg","epic","migration"]}
 {"id":"bd-33vh.1","title":"Update install.sh: Rename 'Git Safety Guard' references to 'DCG'","description":"## Task Overview

Update install.sh to remove all references to 'Git Safety Guard' and consistently use 'DCG' naming.

## Current State (install.sh lines 3933-3940)

```bash
# Install Claude destructive-command guard hook automatically.
#
# This is especially important because ACFS config includes "dangerous mode"
# aliases (e.g., \`cc\`) that can run commands without interactive approvals.
log_detail "Installing Claude Git Safety Guard (PreToolUse hook)"
try_step_eval "Installing Claude Git Safety Guard" \
    "TARGET_USER='$TARGET_USER' TARGET_HOME='$TARGET_HOME' '$ACFS_HOME/scripts/services-setup.sh' --install-claude-guard --yes" || \
    log_warn "Claude Git Safety Guard installation failed (optional)"
```

## Required Changes

1. **Line 3933-3936**: Update comment to reference DCG, not 'destructive-command guard hook'
2. **Line 3937**: Change `log_detail` message from 'Installing Claude Git Safety Guard' to 'Installing DCG (Destructive Command Guard)'
3. **Line 3938**: Change `try_step_eval` step name from 'Installing Claude Git Safety Guard' to 'Installing DCG hook'
4. **Line 3940**: Change `log_warn` message from 'Claude Git Safety Guard installation failed' to 'DCG hook installation failed'

## Why This Matters

- **User Confusion**: Users see 'Git Safety Guard' in install logs but DCG in doctor output
- **Consistency**: All messaging should use 'DCG' to match the actual tool name
- **Accuracy**: The underlying code already calls `configure_dcg()` so naming should match

## Verification

After changes:
```bash
grep -n 'Git Safety Guard' install.sh  # Should return nothing
grep -n 'DCG' install.sh                # Should show the updated lines
```

## Notes

- The `--install-claude-guard` flag in services-setup.sh is still used internally but doesn't need renaming as it's an internal API
- The actual functionality already uses DCG—this is purely a naming/messaging cleanup","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":15,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:01.131493895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:01.131493895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-change","install-script"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.1","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:01.131493895Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33vh.2","title":"Update docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md: Remove git_safety_guard references","description":"## Task Overview

Update docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md to remove references to the old 'Claude Git Safety Guard' and replace with DCG references.

## Current State

The file has two references:

### Line 223 (Gap Analysis Table)
```markdown
| Install Claude Git Safety Guard hook | ❌ No |
```

### Line 245 (Numbered List)
```markdown
12. **Claude Git Safety Guard** hook installation
```

## Required Changes

### Change 1: Line 223
Update the table row to reference DCG:
```markdown
| Install DCG (Destructive Command Guard) hook | ❌ No |
```

### Change 2: Line 245
Update the list item:
```markdown
12. **DCG (Destructive Command Guard)** hook installation
```

## Context

This document analyzes gaps between what the manifest covers vs what install.sh does. The DCG hook installation is still a 'gap' (not manifest-driven), but the naming should be accurate.

## Why This Matters

- **Accuracy**: Documentation should reflect current tooling
- **Searchability**: Users searching for DCG should find relevant docs
- **Consistency**: All ACFS docs should use consistent terminology

## Verification

```bash
grep -n 'Git Safety Guard' docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md  # Should return nothing
grep -n 'DCG' docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md                # Should show updated lines
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":10,"created_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:14.183678098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:14.183678098Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","documentation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33vh.2","depends_on_id":"bd-33vh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:37:14.183678098Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -32,6 +32,7 @@ VERBOSE=false
 REDACT=true
 OUTPUT_BASE="${ACFS_HOME}/support"
 REDACTION_COUNT=0
+DOCTOR_TIMEOUT="${SUPPORT_BUNDLE_DOCTOR_TIMEOUT:-120}"
 
 # ============================================================
 # Parse arguments
@@ -116,7 +117,7 @@ capture_doctor_json() {
 
     if [[ -n "$doctor_script" ]]; then
         log_detail "Running acfs doctor --json ..."
-        if timeout 120 bash "$doctor_script" doctor --json > "$bundle_dir/doctor.json" 2>/dev/null; then
+        if timeout "$DOCTOR_TIMEOUT" bash "$doctor_script" doctor --json > "$bundle_dir/doctor.json" 2>/dev/null; then
             BUNDLE_FILES+=("doctor.json")
             return 0
         else
@@ -313,7 +314,8 @@ redact_file() {
     local file="$1"
 
     # Skip binary files (check first 512 bytes for null bytes)
-    if head -c 512 "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -qP '\x00'; then
+    # -a forces grep to treat input as text (otherwise it silently skips binary data)
+    if head -c 512 "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -qaP '\x00'; then
         return 0
     fi
 
@@ -346,7 +348,7 @@ redact_file() {
     local after_hash
     after_hash=$(md5sum "$file" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}') || return 0
     if [[ "$before_hash" != "$after_hash" ]]; then
-        ((REDACTION_COUNT++))
+        REDACTION_COUNT=$((REDACTION_COUNT + 1))
     fi
 }
 
@@ -365,15 +367,17 @@ redact_bundle() {
     local file_count=0
     while IFS= read -r file; do
         redact_file "$file"
-        ((file_count++))
+        file_count=$((file_count + 1))
     done < <(find "$bundle_dir" -type f \( \
         -name '*.json' -o -name '*.log' -o -name '*.txt' \
         -o -name '*.yaml' -o -name '*.yml' -o -name '*.sh' \
         -o -name '*.zshrc' -o -name '.zshrc' \
         -o -name 'os-release' -o -name 'VERSION' \
         \) 2>/dev/null)
 
-    [[ "$VERBOSE" == "true" ]] && log_detail "Scanned $file_count files, redacted $REDACTION_COUNT"

+        log_detail "Scanned $file_count files, redacted $REDACTION_COUNT"
+    fi
 }
 
 # ============================================================
@@ -400,9 +404,9 @@ main() {
 
     # --- Collect ACFS state files ---
     log_detail "Collecting ACFS state files..."
-    collect_file "$ACFS_HOME/state.json" "$bundle_dir" "state.json"
-    collect_file "$ACFS_HOME/VERSION" "$bundle_dir" "VERSION"

-                ((log_count++))

         [[ "$VERBOSE" == "true" ]] && log_detail "Collected $log_count log files"

-    capture_doctor_json "$bundle_dir"
+    capture_doctor_json "$bundle_dir" || true

+    capture_versions "$bundle_dir" || true
 
     # --- Capture environment ---
     log_detail "Collecting environment info..."
-    capture_env_summary "$bundle_dir"
+    capture_env_summary "$bundle_dir" || true
 
     # --- Collect system info ---
     log_detail "Collecting system info..."
@@ -456,10 +460,8 @@ main() {
 
     # --- Collect configuration ---
     log_detail "Collecting configuration..."
-    collect_file "$HOME/.zshrc" "$bundle_dir/config" ".zshrc"
-    if [[ -f "$ACFS_HOME/acfs.manifest.yaml" ]]; then
-        collect_file "$ACFS_HOME/acfs.manifest.yaml" "$bundle_dir/config" "acfs.manifest.yaml"
-    fi
+    collect_file "$HOME/.zshrc" "$bundle_dir/config" ".zshrc" || true
+    collect_file "$ACFS_HOME/acfs.manifest.yaml" "$bundle_dir/config" "acfs.manifest.yaml" || true
 
     # --- Redact sensitive data ---
     redact_bundle "$bundle_dir"

@@ -0,0 +1,326 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# shellcheck disable=SC2034,SC2317
+# SC2034: YELLOW/VERBOSE/REDACT are used by eval'd functions (indirect use)
+# SC2317: log_* stubs appear unreachable but are called by eval'd functions
+# ============================================================
+# Tests for support-bundle redaction rules (bd-31ps.2.2)
+#
+# Verifies:
+# - Known secret patterns are redacted with <REDACTED:type> markers
+# - Safe values (git SHAs, version strings, paths) are NOT redacted
+# - --no-redact flag disables redaction
+# - Binary files are skipped
+# - Redaction count is tracked
+# - Manifest includes redaction summary
+#
+# Usage: bash tests/test_support_redaction.sh
+# Exit: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
+# ============================================================
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
+SUPPORT_SH="$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/lib/support.sh"
+
+# Test counters
+TESTS_RUN=0
+TESTS_PASSED=0
+TESTS_FAILED=0
+
+# Colors (respects NO_COLOR)
+if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]]; then
+    RED='\033[0;31m'
+    GREEN='\033[0;32m'
+    YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
+    NC='\033[0m'
+else
+    RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' NC=''
+fi
+
+pass() {
+    TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
+    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))
+    printf "${GREEN}PASS${NC} %s\n" "$1"
+}
+
+fail() {
+    TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
+    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))
+    printf "${RED}FAIL${NC} %s\n" "$1"
+    if [[ -n "${2:-}" ]]; then
+        printf "     %s\n" "$2"
+    fi
+}
+
+# Create a temp directory for test fixtures, clean up on exit
+TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d /tmp/acfs_redaction_test_XXXXXX)
+trap 'rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"' EXIT
+
+# ============================================================
+# Load redact_file and redact_bundle from support.sh
+# ============================================================
+# We source support.sh in a controlled way:
+# 1. Pre-define stubs so top-level code and main() become no-ops
+# 2. Source the file to get all function definitions
+# 3. Override any side effects
+
+# Minimal logging stubs (must be defined BEFORE sourcing)
+log_step()    { :; }
+log_section() { :; }
+log_detail()  { :; }
+log_success() { :; }
+log_warn()    { :; }
+log_error()   { :; }
+
+# Source support.sh with --help to make it exit early from arg parsing,
+# but capture its functions. We use a subshell-free approach: define
+# main as a no-op, then source the file with no args.
+# Actually, support.sh runs main "$@" at the end, so we need to
+# prevent that. We'll extract just the function definitions via awk
+# which handles nested braces correctly.
+_extract_functions() {
+    awk '
+    /^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z_0-9]*\(\)/ { inside=1; brace=0; fname=$0 }
+    inside {
+        n = split($0, chars, "")
+        for (i=1; i<=n; i++) {
+            if (chars[i] == "{") brace++
+            if (chars[i] == "}") brace--
+        }
+        print
+        if (brace == 0 && inside) inside=0
+    }
+    ' "$1"
+}
+
+# Extract and eval all function definitions from support.sh
+eval "$(_extract_functions "$SUPPORT_SH")"
+
+REDACT=true
+REDACTION_COUNT=0
+VERBOSE=false
+
+# ============================================================
+# Test helpers
+# ============================================================
+
+# Create a test file with given content, run redaction, return content
+redact_and_read() {
+    local filename="$1"
+    local content="$2"
+    local filepath="$TEST_DIR/$filename"
+    printf '%s' "$content" > "$filepath"
+    REDACTION_COUNT=0
+    redact_file "$filepath"
+    cat "$filepath"
+}
+
+# Assert file content contains a string
+assert_contains() {
+    local test_name="$1"
+    local actual="$2"
+    local expected="$3"
+    if echo "$actual" | grep -qF "$expected"; then
+        pass "$test_name"
+    else
+        fail "$test_name" "Expected to contain: $expected"
+    fi
+}
+
+# Assert file content does NOT contain a string
+assert_not_contains() {
+    local test_name="$1"
+    local actual="$2"
+    local unexpected="$3"
+    if echo "$actual" | grep -qF "$unexpected"; then
+        fail "$test_name" "Should NOT contain: $unexpected"
+    else
+        pass "$test_name"
+    fi
+}
+
+# Assert REDACTION_COUNT equals expected value
+assert_redaction_count() {
+    local test_name="$1"
+    local expected="$2"
+    if [[ "$REDACTION_COUNT" -eq "$expected" ]]; then
+        pass "$test_name"
+    else
+        fail "$test_name" "Expected count=$expected, got count=$REDACTION_COUNT"
+    fi
+}
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: API key patterns
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== API Key Redaction ==="
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "openai.txt" "OPENAI_KEY=sk-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890")
+assert_contains "OpenAI sk- key redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:api_key>"
+assert_not_contains "OpenAI sk- key value removed" "$result" "sk-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890"
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "anthropic.txt" "key: sk-ant-api03-aBcDeFgHiJkLmNoPqRsTuVwXyZ")
+assert_contains "Anthropic sk-ant- key redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:api_key>"
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "aws.txt" "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE")
+assert_contains "AWS access key redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:aws_key>"
+assert_not_contains "AWS key value removed" "$result" "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE"
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: GitHub token patterns
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== GitHub Token Redaction ==="
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "ghp.txt" "GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmn")
+assert_contains "GitHub PAT (ghp_) redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:github_token>"
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "ghs.txt" "token: ghs_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmn")
+assert_contains "GitHub server token (ghs_) redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:github_token>"
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "ghpat.txt" "PAT=github_pat_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZab")
+assert_contains "GitHub fine-grained PAT redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:github_pat>"
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: Other service tokens
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== Service Token Redaction ==="
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "vault.txt" "VAULT_TOKEN=hvs.CAESIJaLm0nOpQrStUvWxYz01234")
+assert_contains "Vault token redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:vault_token>"
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "slack.txt" "SLACK_BOT_TOKEN=xoxb-123456789012-abcdefghijkl")
+assert_contains "Slack bot token redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:slack_token>"
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: Bearer tokens and JWTs
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== Bearer & JWT Redaction ==="
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "bearer.txt" "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.dozjgNryP4J3jVmNHl0w5N_XgL0n3I9PlFUP0THsR8U")
+assert_contains "Bearer token redacted" "$result" "Bearer <REDACTED:bearer>"
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "jwt.txt" "token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.dozjgNryP4J3jVmNHl0w5N_XgL0n3I9PlFUP0THsR8U")
+assert_contains "JWT redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:jwt>"
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: Generic secret patterns (KEY=value)
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== Generic Secret Patterns ==="
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "env_secrets.txt" "API_KEY=mysupersecretvalue123
+SECRET_KEY=anotherlongsecrethere
+ACCESS_TOKEN=verylongtokenvalue99
+PASSWORD=hunter2isnotsafe
+client_secret=abcdefghijklmnop")
+assert_contains "API_KEY redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:API_KEY>"
+assert_contains "SECRET_KEY redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:SECRET_KEY>"
+assert_contains "ACCESS_TOKEN redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:ACCESS_TOKEN>"
+assert_contains "PASSWORD redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED:password>"
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: Safe values NOT redacted (false positive prevention)
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== False Positive Prevention ==="
+
+result=$(redact_and_read "safe.txt" 'git_sha=a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2
+version=1.23.456
+PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin
+TERM=xterm-256color
+status=ok
+name=ubuntu
+HOME=/home/ubuntu')
+assert_not_contains "Git SHA not redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED"
+assert_contains "Git SHA preserved" "$result" "a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2"
+assert_contains "Version preserved" "$result" "1.23.456"
+assert_contains "PATH preserved" "$result" "/usr/local/bin"
+assert_contains "HOME preserved" "$result" "/home/ubuntu"
+
+# Short passwords (< 4 chars) should not be redacted
+result=$(redact_and_read "short_pw.txt" "PASSWORD=abc")
+assert_not_contains "Short password not redacted" "$result" "<REDACTED"
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: Binary file skipping
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== Binary File Handling ==="
+
+# Create a file with null bytes (binary)
+printf 'sk-secretkey1234567890abcdef\x00binary' > "$TEST_DIR/binary.bin"
+REDACTION_COUNT=0
+redact_file "$TEST_DIR/binary.bin"
+assert_redaction_count "Binary file skipped (count=0)" 0
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: Redaction count tracking
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== Redaction Counting ==="
+
+REDACTION_COUNT=0
+printf 'key1=sk-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890\nkey2=ghp_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmn\n' > "$TEST_DIR/multi.txt"
+redact_file "$TEST_DIR/multi.txt"
+assert_redaction_count "Multiple secrets in one file = 1 file redacted" 1
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: redact_bundle walks directory
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== Bundle Redaction ==="
+
+REDACT=true
+REDACTION_COUNT=0
+mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/bundle/logs"
+printf 'token=sk-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890\n' > "$TEST_DIR/bundle/state.json"
+printf 'log: ghp_ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmn\n' > "$TEST_DIR/bundle/logs/install.log"
+printf 'safe content no secrets here\n' > "$TEST_DIR/bundle/clean.txt"
+redact_bundle "$TEST_DIR/bundle"
+
+state_content=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/bundle/state.json")
+log_content=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/bundle/logs/install.log")
+assert_contains "Bundle: state.json redacted" "$state_content" "<REDACTED:api_key>"
+assert_contains "Bundle: install.log redacted" "$log_content" "<REDACTED:github_token>"
+if [[ "$REDACTION_COUNT" -ge 2 ]]; then
+    pass "Bundle: redaction count >= 2"
+else
+    fail "Bundle: redaction count >= 2" "Got $REDACTION_COUNT"
+fi
+
+# ============================================================
+# Tests: --no-redact flag
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "=== --no-redact Flag ==="
+
+REDACT=false
+REDACTION_COUNT=0
+printf 'key=sk-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890\n' > "$TEST_DIR/no_redact.json"
+mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/noredact_bundle"
+cp "$TEST_DIR/no_redact.json" "$TEST_DIR/noredact_bundle/"
+redact_bundle "$TEST_DIR/noredact_bundle"
+nr_content=$(cat "$TEST_DIR/noredact_bundle/no_redact.json")
+assert_contains "no-redact: secret preserved" "$nr_content" "sk-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890"
+assert_redaction_count "no-redact: count stays 0" 0
+
+# ============================================================
+# Summary
+# ============================================================
+echo ""
+echo "========================================"
+printf "Results: %d/%d passed" "$TESTS_PASSED" "$TESTS_RUN"
+if [[ "$TESTS_FAILED" -gt 0 ]]; then
+    printf ", ${RED}%d FAILED${NC}" "$TESTS_FAILED"
+fi
+echo ""
+echo "========================================"
+
+if [[ "$TESTS_FAILED" -gt 0 ]]; then
+    exit 1
+fi
+exit 0
